title: tragia paraguariensis var. macrophylla
intro: Tragia paraguariensis var. macrophylla, also known as the Macrophylla Horseweed or Tragia, is a plant species in the Euphorbiaceae family. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant is rich in flavonoids, saponins, and other bioactive compounds, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing pain, inflammation, and improving skin health. In herbal preparations, Tragia paraguariensis var. macrophylla is often used as a decoction or infusion, and its leaves and roots are dried and used as a medicinal tea or added to other herbal remedies.
